GitHub Actions workflow for building and storing bootloader images on push or pull request to the main branch.

# Purpose
This GitHub Actions workflow automates the process of building a bootloader for a specified board. It triggers on pushes and pull requests to the `main` branch, specifically when changes occur in the `.github/workflows/build-bootloader.yml`, `platform/**`, or `third_party/**` paths. The workflow runs on an `ubuntu-24.04` environment using a specified Docker container image. It includes several steps: marking the GitHub workspace as safe, checking out the repository with submodules, installing Python dependencies, obtaining the platform name, configuring the bootloader, building the bootloader, and finally storing the resulting bootloader images as artifacts. The use of a matrix strategy allows the workflow to target different boards, with the current configuration set to build for the "asterix" board.
